title: Band Formation
description: John Rzeznik and Robby Takac form the Goo Goo Dolls in Buffalo, New York, initially performing as a cover band under the name 'The Sex Maggots' before a venue owner insists on a name change. They recruit drummer George Tutuska to complete the lineup.

title: First EP Released
description: The band self-releases their debut EP on Celluloid Records, establishing themselves in the Buffalo punk scene with raw, energetic tracks.

title: Jed Released
description: The Goo Goo Dolls release their debut full-length album Jed on Metal Blade Records, showcasing their Replacements-influenced punk rock sound.

title: Hold Me Up Released
description: The band releases their second album Hold Me Up, continuing their punk-pop trajectory while gaining more regional attention and touring extensively.

title: Superstar Car Wash Released
description: Superstar Car Wash, their third album and first on Warner Bros. Records, marks the beginning of their transition toward a more polished alternative rock sound.

title: A Boy Named Goo Released
description: A Boy Named Goo becomes the band's commercial breakthrough, propelled by the hit single 'Name' which reaches #5 on the Billboard Hot 100. The album goes double platinum.

title: George Tutuska Departs
description: Original drummer George Tutuska leaves the band amid the recording sessions for A Boy Named Goo. He is replaced by Mike Malinin, who will remain the band's drummer for nearly two decades.

title: 'Iris' Released on City of Angels Soundtrack
description: 'Iris,' written by Rzeznik for the film City of Angels, becomes one of the biggest songs of the decade. It spends 18 weeks at #1 on the Hot 100 Airplay chart and is later named Billboard's #1 Pop Song of 1992-2012.

title: Dizzy Up the Girl Released
description: Dizzy Up the Girl becomes the band's best-selling album, reaching quadruple platinum status. It spawns hit singles 'Slide,' 'Black Balloon,' and 'Broadway' alongside the already-massive 'Iris.'

title: Grammy Nominations for 'Iris'
description: The Goo Goo Dolls receive Grammy nominations for Record of the Year, Best Pop Performance by Duo or Group, and Best Rock Song for 'Iris,' cementing the song's status as a cultural touchstone.

title: Gutterflower Released
description: Gutterflower continues the band's mainstream success with singles 'Here Is Gone' and 'Sympathy.' The album reaches platinum status.

title: Massive Hometown Concert in Buffalo
description: The Goo Goo Dolls perform a landmark free Independence Day concert in Buffalo, New York, drawing an estimated 60,000 fans. The performance is released as a live album.

title: Let Love In Released
description: Let Love In features the hit singles 'Better Days,' 'Give a Little Bit' (a Supertramp cover), and 'Stay with You,' marking the band's continued radio relevance in the 2000s.

title: Songwriters Hall of Fame
description: John Rzeznik is inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame, recognizing his contributions as one of the most successful rock songwriters of the 1990s and 2000s.

title: Something for the Rest of Us Released
description: The band's eighth studio album Something for the Rest of Us explores darker, more introspective themes while maintaining their signature melodic approach.

title: 'Iris' Named #1 Pop Song of Two Decades
description: Billboard names 'Iris' the #1 Pop Song on its 'Top 100 Pop Songs 1992-2012' chart, confirming the song's enduring legacy across two decades of popular music.

title: Mike Malinin Departs
description: After 18 years as the band's drummer, Mike Malinin departs the Goo Goo Dolls. He is subsequently replaced by Craig Macintyre.

title: Magnetic Released
description: Magnetic represents a sonic shift with more synth-driven production and electronic textures layered over the band's guitar-driven foundation.

title: Boxes Released
description: Boxes continues the band's exploration of modern production techniques, with singles 'So Alive' and 'Over and Over' receiving solid radio play.

title: Miracle Pill Released
description: Miracle Pill marks the band's twelfth studio album, featuring a mix of anthemic rock and introspective ballads that showcase the continued evolution of Rzeznik's songwriting.

title: Chaos in Bloom Released
description: Chaos in Bloom, the band's thirteenth studio album, is self-produced by Rzeznik for the first time and embraces a more raw, guitar-forward sound reminiscent of their earlier work.
